FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class MeasuredStackVirtualLayout

Namespace
FluentGpu.Scene
Assembly
FluentGpu.Scene.dll

The Fenwick variable-extent list behind the IMeasuredVirtualLayout seam (E11-L0): every row starts at Estimate, gets corrected to its measured extent on realize, and the layout engine re-pins the scroll anchor across corrections. Functionally the engine's legacy Layout = null variable path (kept for compatibility), but USER-REACHABLE: compose it, subclass the idea, or build your own measured layout on the same contract. STATEFUL (owns an ExtentTable) — create ONCE and reuse across renders (hoist in a UseMemo); the table self-rebuilds only on item-count change.

public sealed class MeasuredStackVirtualLayout : IMeasuredVirtualLayout, IVirtualLayout

OffsetOf(int, float)

Content-space main-axis offset of item index (prefix sum over corrected extents).

Parameters

index int
crossSize float

Returns

float

SetMeasured(int, float, float)

Correct item index's main-axis extent to its measured value (estimate-then-correct). Called by the layout engine for every realized row at arrange time; O(log n), allocation-free.

public void SetMeasured(int index, float mainExtent, float crossSize)

Parameters

index int
mainExtent float
crossSize float

Window(int, float, float, float, int, out int, out int)

The [first,last) item range to realize for scrollOffset (incl. overscan).

n int
cross float

overscan int
first int
last int
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
